mission.title: n8n: Slack Trigger node throws errors on `File Shared` event
mission.description: Slack Trigger node throws errors on `File Shared` event. This issue affects 8+ users.

steps.title: Identify n8n troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your n8n deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n n8n -l app.kubernetes.io/name=n8n
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=n8n -n n8n --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review n8n configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant n8n configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n n8n -l app.kubernetes.io/name=n8n
kubectl get configmap -n n8n -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=n8n
```
### Bug Description

Slack Trigger node throws erros when listening on `File Shared` event:

`TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'channel') at WebhookContext.webhook

steps.title: Apply the fix for Slack Trigger node throws errors on `File Shared` event
steps.description:
## Summary

This PR fixes a critical bug in the Slack Trigger node where `file_share` events were throwing `TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'channel')` errors. The issue occurred because the `file_share` event structure in Slack uses a `channel_id` attribute that wasn't being handled by the existing channel detection logic.

- Added support for `req.body.event.channel_id` as a fallback option when determining the event channel
- Improved null safety with optional

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Confirm Slack Trigger node throws errors on `File Shared`… is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=n8n -n n8n --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n n8n --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:
## Summary

This PR fixes a critical bug in the Slack Trigger node where `file_share` events were throwing `TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'channel')` errors. The issue occurred because the `file_share` event structure in Slack uses a `channel_id` attribute that wasn't being handled by the existing channel detection logic.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with n8n installed or the issue environment reproducible.
